message:
fix(mobile): improve touch targets and action drawer UX

Comprehensive mobile UX improvements focusing on WCAG 2.5.5 compliance,
touch target sizing, and responsive behavior refinements.

ActionDrawer.tsx:
- Changed default snap point from 'half' to 'full' for screens ≤900px
  so all actions are immediately accessible without dragging
- Increased minHeight from 35% to 40% and maxHeight from 85% to 92%
  for better content visibility

BottomSheet.tsx:
- Refined spring animation parameters for more natural feel
- Improved gesture handling for smoother drag interactions

Overlay.tsx:
- Enhanced mobile overlay presentation
- Better safe-area-inset handling for notched devices

mobile-ux.css (major additions):
- WCAG 2.5.5 touch targets: Labels wrapping checkboxes now provide
  48px minimum touch height, making checkboxes easier to tap
- Active state feedback on checkbox/radio labels with background change
- Larger bottom sheet handle (44px min-height, 40x5px pill)
- Natural iOS elastic overscroll instead of contained behavior
- Empty states designed per Material Design 3 guidelines
- Improved skeleton loading states for mobile
- Better focus ring visibility for accessibility

action-drawer.css:
- Enhanced visual hierarchy for action groups
- Improved spacing and typography for mobile screens
- Better icon alignment and sizing

fab.css:
- Refined FAB positioning for various screen sizes
- Improved shadow and elevation
- Better touch feedback states

mobile-overrides.css:
- Additional media query refinements
- Safe area adjustments for home indicator

swipe-indicators.css:
- Enhanced swipe gesture hints
- Better animation timing

These changes improve the mobile experience for touch devices while
maintaining full accessibility compliance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
